item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, the Great Seventeen-Toed Hairy Prairie Hidebehind. Of McBroom's Zoo fame. I wonder if it’s bottom half sheered off because of that time it turned around so fast trying to hide behind itself.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why you don’t buy cheap stand arrows

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *Teleports behind you*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Partial re-embodiment sold me on this one. Subtle touch which really leaves a number of disturbing possibilities to consider.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
This is in violation of our Criticism Policy, which is required reading before joining the site. Specifically:

Your posts must contain some kind of content: "Meh" on its own does not count as content, because it does not help the author improve their work and is no more informative than a downvote. An emoticon, an insulting macro, or "lol" is not content; it's spam. Stating "I agree with what so-and-so said" is sufficient because it gives support to existing feedback.

and:

In short: Don't be an asshole.

Please keep this in mind for your future critiques.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is it terrible?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Terrible. Downvote.

item.title: Necropost? -\_ 😏_/-
item.content:encoded:
God, that's creepy.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the description, it says it bears no reflection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do I feel like I'm browsing the camera roll of a serial killer when I look through those?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh… just an inferior version of SCP-650

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2372 casts no shadow

The "purple warthog laying down" casts a shadow though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay.

D-Class personnel must enter the containment chamber alone because, yes, the main reason they would need to do so is for testing; SCP-2373 can only be observed in its active state while behind one person.

Precisely: the main reason. How about cleaning, or checking, or whatever? In this case, shouldn't there be two D-Class to avoid activating the skip? I'd suggest: D-Class personnel not destined for testing are to enter the containment chamber alone. What say you?

For some reason, in my mind 'relocation' implies teleportation, yes. How about using the verb 'to reposition (oneself)' instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that's more for the Author to decide. As it stands, the rating is in the positives. My opinion, too, is that it could do with some extent of expansion as to its supposed origins. I like the footnote though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To address your questions:

1. D-Class personnel must enter the containment chamber alone because, yes, the main reason they would need to do so is for testing; SCP-2373 can only be observed in its active state while behind one person.

This effect is perpetuated until SCP-2373 is outwardly observed by a third party

So, if another D-Class personnel is in the containment chamber, it would be in its dormant state. It's another excuse to be spooky by ensuring that no-one can directly observe it in its active state.

2. That's right, food wouldn't be required, and it would support the hypothesis mentioned in the article.

3. In this case, "relocate" just means "travel by floating very quickly". It can't teleport; it might have been able to do so when it was a ghost, but you decide that.

And to address Le Roof:

To make its relocation impossible, every single room in every building in the entire world would have to have at least two people in it.

It's not stated in the article, but since SCP-2373 can't teleport, and doesn't possess the power to obliterate everything in its path (including Site-19's various uber-reinforced blast doors, including the airlock doors to its containment chamber), then it won't be able to travel to an environment without two people in it.

I'm also assuming that Sensei Le Roof got "teleportation" from "relocation"… right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spooky indeed, but a few things are a bit puzzling:

No fewer than two non-D-Class personnel are to enter SCP-2373's containment chamber. D-Class personnel are to enter the containment chamber alone.

D-Class personnel are used for testing, but why must they always be alone in there? Not clear, I think…

Just to be clear: SCP-2373 is not alive, and therefore does not need comfort and food - did I get that right?

And, most importantly:

This effect is perpetuated until SCP-2373 is outwardly observed by a third party, attacked, or obstructed by a solid, where it will relocate to an environment in which one or no human individuals are present. If this is not possible, SCP-2373 will re-enter its dormant state, facing against the nearest wall, and away from all human individuals.

Is this thing capable of teleportation? This is what I understand by "relocate"… In which case, and also taking into consideration Sensei Le Roof's comment:

Do you seriously believe this would ever not be possible? Hell, I spend about 90% of my free time by myself. To make its relocation impossible, every single room in every building in the entire world would have to have at least two people in it.

…SCP-2373 is nigh impossible to contain. Or am I particularly thick today? ;>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Both of the used images have been released under CC, so yes, the end image is fine both legally and under our policy, and no permission is needed. If the images weren't released under that or similar license, just mashing them up together wouldn't magically remove the copyrights from them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does this image meet site Image Use Policy? Specifically, would the permission of the these flickr users be required, or would a mash-up of their respective works be considered fair use?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I'm all for that. I'm just trying to point to some kind of backstory here. It's fine that it's listed as Euclid. I just think it would go further with some support, underlying why it might be difficult to contain or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Euclid SCPs are usually defined by unpredictability and inability to "just lock it up and expect nothing to happen". Once someone sees it, its anomalous effect is invoked. Granted that it can follow the subject who observed it, containment breach is a rather real concern in containing this SCP.

Object Classes are not really based on what an SCP does. It's more of "How do I lock it away and make normal society don't know about it?" (As far as my head-canon of Object Classes are concerned)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And like I said, why is it Euclid? Did it do something to deserve that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 because this is one of my personal phobias played to perfection, and that bit about there apparently being something out there that didn't like being followed, and could do that to a ghost-thing…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mind simple, but there just isn't enough to this, and the concept, while workable (good, even), isn't stellar enough to hold itself up without more material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Where your eyes don't go, a filthy scarecrow waves its broomstick arms and does a parody of each unconscious thing you do.

Unintentional (?) TMBG reference aside, there's not much to this and the "it's super-fast" part was a little off-key, so to speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image is an outstanding part of what appears to be a collection of comparatively innocent photographs. Really, I don't know if it's real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually like this, and am able to find several hooks. First hook: the article leaves you curious as to this thing's backstory. Hook the second: there is something out there which can grab what it effectively a ghost that can become intangible and force that ghost to become permanently tangible, and may or may not own a buzzsaw. Final and most obvious hook: it could be right behind you and you wouldn't know. All this adds up to an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God, is that an actual corpse? I really wish I didn't click on that image. Please put a warning on that kind of link next time. It's very unsettling.

As for the SCP, it has a nice eerie vibe. I know I'd be freaked out having that behind me, just knowing it's there whether or not I could see it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Living scips almost always default to Euclid, especially something like this that we don't fully understand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Spoopy and simple. I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doesn't have a hook to it. At its base - it's a ghost that follows you around.
Good tone - punctual and neutral. I didn't end up questioning the text itself, so not plot-holes as far as I can tell.

Just not interesting, it's basic. I'd support it with some kind of backstory. Why is this listed as Euclid? Did it previously traumatize an entire village or something? You can definitely add something like a story to support it. Great picture, though a little sluggish on the left there (wall looks torn), it works just fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like a living shadow. Spooky. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another question: does the same reaction occur when a person looks at themselves in a mirror? Or does it just relocate limbs and such so it can temporarily fit behind the person's back without being seen? Or does it shift right outside the view of the mirror?

I really like brief (but concise) SCP documents, and this worked out pretty well.
It's nice to see this out of the forums and on the main-list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, the image is too pixelated to make out. It looks like a purple warthog laying down to me. The original image was actually really neat, I would have liked to see it in better quality.

The article itself didnt grab me, made me think of SCP-372, except spookier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to ask…

it will relocate to an environment in which one or no human individuals are present. If this is not possible

Do you seriously believe this would ever not be possible? Hell, I spend about 90% of my free time by myself. To make its relocation impossible, every single room in every building in the entire world would have to have at least two people in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
#no-vote, I guess. It's competently written, and the implications in the last paragraph were pretty neat in my opinion, but other than that, it didn't really creep me out or anything, which I'm guessing was the intended purpose.

On a side note, what actually is that in the picture? I mean, I'm assuming from the picture you linked in here it's a corpse (also not to be presumptuous, but it'd be nice if you specified that before I clicked it [by which I mean the link in this thread]…), but I'm not really sure.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an anomalous person and there isn't much of a story or hook. But the image portrayed gave me a good shudder.

No vote. Good start, but I'd like to see more done with this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Och, I wasn't aware of this. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If a single human subject enters within SCP-2373's direct line of sight, SCP-2373 will animate > via telekinetic suspension, swiftly relocating itself directly behind the subject.

So… … its basically SCP-650.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While it's well described, I'm not seeing much beyond "spooky hairy tortured guy". If there is a story beyond that, which I'm sure there is, you may want to work on bringing it out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey all. I'm trying this one out with the mainsite, seeing as I can't envisage any other way to extend the interest for this idea without completely detracting from its intended simplicity.

Images are a mashup of

https://www.flickr.com/photos/mattcameasarat/5108940725/in/photostream/ (BIG WARNING: NSFW. I don't know if this is an actual corpse, but I'm not taking any chances. I might just remove this link if it becomes too much of an issue.)

and

https://www.flickr.com/photos/jonny2love/3996042726

Edit: Can everyone see the image correctly? It's not working for me, but this issue has arisen before, so I feel the need to confirm that it's just at my end.

Edit Edit: Warning added. I also completely forgot to thank Doctor Cimmerian and Patrick Whelan for feedback in the forums. Thanks, guys!
